element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Wall-type ground interaction, different physics model
Early bosses have multiple hit requirements; later bosses (Snake, Gargamel) present higher difficulty
Mandatory stage, cannot be bypassed by debug features
Empty ground type, non-mandatory content; Cicogne and Riviere stages

typical_tension_triggers: Late-game bosses with reduced health (Snake, Gargamel)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive difficulty escalation from flat platforming through physics-variation stages to mandatory boss gauntlet and final stairs challenge

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Bonus stages accessible but not mandatory; can be skipped in progression
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Full world map exists but camera panning obscures southeast section—encourages exploration of edge areas

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory flat platforming with basic side-scrolling mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of physics variation (slippery chariot, wall-based luge) alongside increasing boss difficulty
difficulty_curve.late_game: Mandatory stairs stage; late bosses (Snake, Gargamel) present significant one-hit-death threat
notable_spikes: First two bosses appear early but increase in hit requirements
Stairs stage (Escalier) acts as late-game difficulty gate
Snake and Gargamel bosses represent final challenge tier

notable_level_patterns.description: Stages systematically introduce different ground physics: flat (plat), slippery flat (glissant plat), and wall-based (mur). Bonus stages feature empty ground (vide). This creates rhythm through mechanical novelty rather than spatial novelty.

Different Smurf characters appear in stages, with Luge stage using a character named 'Luge' and bonus stages displaying no character name. This suggests character-stage pairing affects mechanics or context.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Luge stage (character 'Luge'), bonus stages (no character display)

notable_level_patterns.description: Stairs stage (Escalier) is explicitly cannot be bypassed, even with debug features enabled. Functions as a skill checkpoint before late-game content.

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - The Smurfs SNES article)
